itemListElement.reviewBody:
My experience with a well-known co-ed CBSE school in Alipore has been deeply disappointing, particularly regarding the mindset and behavior of some of the teachers toward parents. It is disheartening to see how the school chooses to focus on trivial matters such as parents’ attire during drop-off and pick-up rather than fostering a progressive and inclusive environment.

I was appalled when a teacher Mrs. Gomes had the audacity to comment on my choice of wearing sports leggings, stating that they appeared “tight” and could give male students and staff the “wrong impression” about me. When I questioned her about what values they were teaching the boys, her response was even more concerning—she openly admitted that they do not teach their boys anything about respect or perception but instead focus on teaching the girls how to dress “appropriately.” Such a mindset is not only outdated but also alarming for a school that should be nurturing equality and respect among students.

Initially, I thought this was an isolated incident, but the following academic year proved otherwise. Another teacher took it upon herself to comment on the length of my dress’s neckline and suggested that knee-length dresses were “inappropriate.” The situation escalated further when, upon anonymously addressing this issue with the management, instead of acknowledging the problem, they issued an official dress code circular for parents. This move confirmed how deeply ingrained their regressive mindset is, making it clear that this is not the kind of environment I want my child to grow up in.

Adding to this unpleasant experience, one particular staff member Ms. Saddique deserves mention for her unwelcoming and judgmental attitude. Her constant stares at parents, as if scanning them from head to toe, and complete lack of warmth make interactions uncomfortable. A teacher should be approachable and kind, but her demeanor suggests otherwise.

Facilities & Infrastructure

Apart from these serious cultural issues, the school also falls short in terms of basic infrastructure and facilities. There is no playground or auditorium, limiting students’ opportunities for outdoor activities, sports, and large-scale school events. A well-rounded education should foster both academic and extracurricular development, but this institution fails to provide even these fundamental spaces.

The school lacks proper air conditioning, making classrooms unbearably hot during summer. Despite having classrooms on higher floors, students are not allowed to use the lift, even if their classroom is on the seventh floor. This rigid and impractical rule raises serious concerns about the students’ well-being.

Another shocking issue is the state of the toilets, which are often dirty and poorly maintained. There have been instances where the same poop stain remained for two consecutive days, highlighting the school’s lack of hygiene and cleanliness. For an institution that claims to provide quality education, such neglect of basic sanitation is completely unacceptable.

Adding to the frustration, parents are unnecessarily harassed when it comes to collecting school essentials. Unlike reputed schools that ensure a smooth distribution process on campus, this school forces parents to collect uniforms and books from different parts of the city, making it an extremely inconvenient and time-consuming process. Such poor management reflects a lack of consideration for parents’ time and effort.

Another major issue is the lack of proper parking facilities, which creates a chaotic and unsafe situation during drop-off and pick-up. Parents are often forced to park illegally in front of the school, and with police frequently cracking down on such violations, it becomes extremely stressful. In many instances, parents have no option but to hurriedly hop into moving vehicles, which is not only inconvenient but also highly unsafe for both children and parents.

Regressive Gender Segregation

Moreover, the school enforces unnecessary segregation between boys and girls, which has negatively impacted my child’s ability to interact confidently with boys. For example, during dispersal, they have separate queues for boys and girls, reinforcing an unnatural divide at an age when children should be encouraged to socialize freely without biases. It is absurd to expect a four-year-old boy to eve-tease a four-year-old girl, and yet the school’s policies reflect such an unfounded fear. This has led to my daughter developing hesitation and anxiety around boys, which is concerning for her social development.

Final Thoughts

While the teaching methods in terms of academics are commendable, a school is about much more than just studies. The values and environment it fosters play a crucial role in shaping young minds, and sadly, this institution fails in this regard.

I would not recommend this school to parents who value progressive thinking, safety, hygiene, and a nurturing environment for their children.

My daughter study in lakshipat singhania academy... My daughter always say i love this school.. school is wonderful... Please join lakshipat singhania academy..it is wonderful 😊😊
Thank you...

Lakshmipat Singhania Academy is not just a school. It's a heaven and haven, for the nurturing, education and enlightenment of budding minds. I am a journalist now, at The Times and doing quite well for myself. Had I not been an LSA-student, I would probably still be pulling at straws here and there to gain semblance and balance in life. The teachers here become friends in no time, and by the end of one's school mark, they leave an indelible positive influence on each and every student. I was a boy when stepped through gate number 1 at 12B Alipore Road, and I left as a man, looking back at more than a decade of learning, and growing.

Lakshmipat Singhania Academy provides extensive opportunities to all students in order to develop in their varied areas of interest. A holistic approach is adopted by the school along with individual attention, which is given to every student. Diverse needs of all students are catered to, by a highly qualified and extremely caring faculty. The environment of learning provided by the school nurtures each child to the best of their potential.
A good school with a caring attitude and manifold experiential activities and workshops for children teachers and parents.
